zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk於1256年出生在英國威爾斯萊可哈姆可時，他的父親Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore26歲，母親Maud de Braose32歲。他和Lucy de la Wafre至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1326年8月3日在英國英格蘭米德萊可堂爾 本 倫敦享年70歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾維格莫 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk was born in 1256, in Chirk, Wrexham, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore, was 26 and his mother, Maud de Braose, was 32. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Lucy de la Wafre. He died on 3 August 1326, in Tower of London, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, England.

fr.Chirk, Wrexham, Wales, United Kingdom: Chirk, Wrexham, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk est né en 1256, à Chirk, Wrexham, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Maud de Braose, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Lucy de la Wafre. Il est décédé le 3 août 1326, à Tower of London, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.Tower of London, England: Tower of London, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk 1256 in Chirk, Wrexham, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore 26 und seine Mutter Maud de Braose 32. Er hatte mit Lucy de la Wafre mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 3. August 1326 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Tower of London, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, England begraben.

Morì il 3 agosto 1326, nel Tower of London, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, England.

ko.Tower of London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 타워 프 런던
ko.lifeSummary: Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk(이)가 1256년 영국 웨일스 레스함 치르크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Braose(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Lucy de la Wafre(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1326년 8월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 타워 프 런던에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어 카스틀에 묻혔다.

ru.Tower of London, England: тоуир оф лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk родился в 1256 г. в местности чирк, рексхам, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Braose, 32. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Lucy de la Wafre. Он умер 3 августа 1326 г. в местности тоуир оф лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности вигмор кастл, хирефордшир, Англия.
